danger_signals.visual_form: Incoming attack sprites from enemy entities
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to player character requiring evasion or defense

danger_signals.visual_form: Large, distinctly-designed enemy sprite occupying significant screen space
danger_signals.meaning: High-difficulty encounter ahead; escalated danger state

danger_signals.visual_form: Character sprite knockback/flash animation; health indicator depletion
danger_signals.meaning: Player has been hit; health status degrading

safety_signals.meaning: Opportunity to strengthen offense or gain temporary invulnerability

safety_signals.visual_form: CPU-controlled ally sprite present on screen or selectable via magic possession
safety_signals.meaning: Support available; can be thrown at enemies for massive damage or possessed for unique abilities

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Ofuda talismans (cards) - traditional Japanese spiritual seals
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Japanese supernatural/shrine aesthetics (based on Japanese title 'Kiki Kaikai: Mysterious Ghost World')

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Diverse enemy sprite designs representing supernatural/yokai entities
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Nighttime/moonlit setting (Japanese title references 'Moonlit Night'); ghostly/supernatural visual themes

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Distinct character designs for each unique ally with individual attack signatures
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ally appears alongside Pocky in promotional/menu art; subordinate positioning visually

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Partner character sprite rendered with AI control behavior
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ally follows programmed attack patterns; attacks automatically without player input for second controller

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Two distinct character sprites on screen with independent animation states
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player 2 character responds to second controller input; cooperation enables throwing mechanics and magic possession

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual transformation of Pocky sprite to reflect temporary possession state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player gains unique ability tied to possessed character; attack patterns and movement change

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ally sprite travels toward enemy in ballistic arc
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Massive damage dealt to target; thrown character becomes temporarily inactive (dead) until respawn

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Menu option text; regional UI variation in options screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game parameters adjust (enemy density, attack patterns, damage scaling)

environmental_storytelling.example: Japanese title 'Tsukiyo' (moonlit night) and 'Tsukiyo Soushi' (moonlit night chronicles) embed temporal context; nighttime aesthetic creates supernatural/ghost world framing without explicit narrative

environmental_storytelling.technique: Character costume/clothing as protective state communication
environmental_storytelling.example: New clothes pickups visually change Pocky's appearance and provide damage protection; visual armor represents mechanical invulnerability

environmental_storytelling.technique: Talisman/card attack system as cultural world-building
environmental_storytelling.example: Ofuda talismans (spiritual seals used in Shinto practice) serve as attack projectiles; visual design grounds game in Japanese supernatural/spiritual framework without explicit exposition

environmental_storytelling.technique: Ally subordination through visual hierarchy and UI
environmental_storytelling.example: Player 2 character rendered as supporting role in promotional art and menu screens; reviews note they act as 'mere subordinates to Pocky,' communicated through sprite positioning and possession mechanics

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Throws ofuda talismans in eight directions; free movement in top-down space; can pick up power-ups and clothing
Visually transforms; gains unique ability tied to possessed ally character (attack pattern and movement change)
Knockback animation; sprite flash; health meter depletes
Attacks with unique ability; possessable by Pocky via magic; can be thrown at enemies for massive damage
Temporarily removed from field; respawns after short delay; indicated visually by absence from screen
Attack Pocky with projectiles or melee; move according to behavior patterns; affected by partner throwables (massive damage)
Enlarged sprite occupying significant screen real estate; more complex attack patterns; stage-defining threat

notes: Pocky & Rocky 2 is a top-down shoot-em-up with Japanese supernatural (yokai/ghost) theming. The manifestation layer primarily communicates through: (1) attack/defense visual feedback (projectiles, knockback, health depletion), (2) power-up and protective clothing sprites as safety/strength signals, (3) distinct ally characters with unique visual designs and attack signatures, (4) possession and throwing mechanics that visually alter game state, and (5) nocturnal/supernatural environmental aesthetic that frames the entire world as a 'mysterious ghost world.' The game uses no explicit faction conflict narrative but establishes opposition through enemy sprite diversity and protagonist-ally hierarchy. Regional UI differences (JP 'Rank' vs. International 'Difficulty', controller button color changes, title card design) do not affect core manifestation systems. The core world-state-to-visual mapping centers on multiplayer cooperation: single-player shows CPU ally behavior; multiplayer shows human control; magic possession shows character transformation; ally throwing shows temporary removal and massive damage impact.
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Pocky & Rocky 2 article)
